english: South African red bush tea (flavoured, packaged)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general rooibos tea entry (FOODON_03301103); no specific entry for flavoured packaged RTD rooibos tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers rooibos tea generically; no specific facet for flavoured or packaged RTD variant. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for flavoured packaged rooibos tea. SNOMED CT anchor omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve packaged rooibos tea is approximately 250 ml (250 g), providing about 88 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Rooibos Tea (Packaged) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Flavoured Rooibos Tea (Packaged), record ID D09020031. Values preserved as provided. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamins, and other micronutrients not reported by HPB for this entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for sweetened herbal tea / low-sugar beverage
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for flavoured packaged rooibos tea with 0 g sugar and 8.66 g carbohydrate per 100 g. GI estimated at approximately 40 (low) by analogy with lightly sweetened herbal teas and dilute carbohydrate beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. The carbohydrate content (8.66 g/100 g) with 0 g sugar is atypical and may reflect non-sugar polysaccharides or natural flavouring compounds from rooibos extract; these are expected to have a low glycaemic impact. GL per 250 g serving = (40 × 8.66 × 2.5) / 100 = 8.66 rounded to 8.7. This estimate should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (57 mg/100 g) and zero added sugar; no SSB or high-sodium gate triggered
hypertension.guidance: With sodium at 57 mg per 100 ml and no added sugar, this beverage is suitable for individuals managing hypertension; rooibos polyphenols (chrysoeriol, orientin) have preliminary evidence for ACE-inhibitory activity that may support blood pressure regulation, though clinical confirmation is needed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar, low GI (~40), and rooibos aspalathin with evidence for insulin-sensitising effects
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains 0 g sugar and has an estimated low GI (~40), making it appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the 8.66 g carbohydrate per 100 ml warrants label verification to confirm the absence of added sugars or caloric sweeteners, and portion size should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, low sodium, and rooibos polyphenols with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Flavoured packaged rooibos tea is free of fat and saturated fat, and its polyphenol content (aspalathin, nothofagin, luteolin) has been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved lipid profiles in preliminary studies, supporting its suitability as a heart-friendly beverage when consumed without added sugar.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Rooibos polyphenols with documented anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Rooibos tea is rich in aspalathin and nothofagin, which have demonstrated anti-inflammatory activity in vitro; zero sugar and zero fat mean no pro-inflammatory dietary triggers are present, making this a suitable beverage for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis, though polyphenol concentration in packaged RTD formats may be lower than freshly brewed rooibos.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Rooibos Tea (Packaged) is a ready-to-drink (RTD), caffeine-free herbal beverage produced from Aspalathus linearis — commonly known as rooibos or red bush — a shrub indigenous to the Cederberg mountain region of the Western Cape, South Africa. The plant has been harvested and consumed by the Khoisan people for centuries, and rooibos tea gained international commercial popularity in the late 20th century as a caffeine-free alternative to black and green tea. The packaged RTD format is produced by brewing rooibos leaves or extract, filtering the infusion, blending in natural flavourings (commonly fruit, floral, or spice notes), and sealing in bottles or cans of typically 250–330 ml for extended shelf life without refrigeration. The resulting beverage has a smooth, naturally sweet, mildly earthy flavour profile with no bitterness or astringency — a characteristic that distinguishes rooibos from tannin-rich black teas. The added flavourings introduce fruity or floral top notes, making the drink refreshing and palatable without the need for added sugar. Nutritionally, this product provides 35 kcal per 100 ml, 0.23 g protein, 0 g fat, and 8.66 g carbohydrate with 0 g sugar as reported by the HPB Singapore Foodo database (D09020031). The 8.66 g carbohydrate with 0 g sugar is an atypical profile that may reflect non-sugar polysaccharides or soluble fibre-like compounds from rooibos extract or natural flavouring agents; consumers should verify the product label to confirm no added sugars or caloric sweeteners are present. Sodium is 57 mg per 100 ml, which is low. Rooibos is uniquely rich in the C-glucosyl dihydrochalcone aspalathin — a polyphenol found exclusively in Aspalathus linearis — along with nothofagin, orientin, isoorientin, luteolin, and chrysoeriol. These compounds have demonstrated antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and preliminary insulin-sensitising and ACE-inhibitory effects in preclinical and early clinical research. Clinically, this beverage is low-concern for hypertension (sodium 57 mg/100 ml, no SSB classification), Type 2 Diabetes (zero sugar, estimated low GI ~40, aspalathin with insulin-sensitising evidence), and cardiovascular disease (zero fat, zero saturated fat, polyphenol-rich). It is naturally caffeine-free, making it appropriate for caffeine-sensitive individuals, pregnant women, children, and those with anxiety or sleep disorders. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Polyphenol concentration in commercial RTD formats may be lower than freshly brewed loose-leaf rooibos due to dilution and processing; consumers seeking therapeutic polyphenol intake should prefer freshly brewed preparations.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D09020031), which is an approved primary source for Singapore food records. The food identity is unambiguous. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence reduced for GI component only) but all other fields are grounded in the approved source.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D09020031); rooibos tea is a water-based herbal infusion and contains no fat by formulation — confirmed zero. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; however, the 8.66 g carbohydrate with 0 g sugar is atypical for a flavoured beverage and warrants label verification to confirm no added sugars or caloric sweeteners are present. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. Saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with 0 g total fat — confirmed zero by formulation. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data for this packaged flavoured rooibos tea are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D09020031 as of 2026. GI value of 40 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database for lightly sweetened herbal teas; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. GL per 250 g serving calculated as (40 × 8.66 × 2.5) / 100 = 8.66, rounded to 8.7. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 and FoodEx2 A0FKP are closest_parent matches for rooibos tea; no exact codes exist for the flavoured packaged RTD variant. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory local-language names apply under G6 multilingual rules.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Expanded natural_language_description to improve LLM readiness for Q6.

Iteration 3: Substantially expanded natural_language_description to comprehensively cover preparation method, sensory profile (taste, texture, aroma), cultural and historical context (Khoisan people, Cederberg region, international commercialisation), clinical relevance for all three required health conditions, polyphenol profile detail (aspalathin exclusivity to rooibos, nothofagin, orientin, luteolin, chrysoeriol), suitability for caffeine-sensitive populations, and RTD vs freshly brewed polyphenol concentration caveat. Added additional common user queries and ingredient synonyms to further strengthen Q6 and Q8.
